name: Altruism
rulesText: After playing this mood — If the discard pile has at least one card in it, this mood's value becomes [6][1]. Then starting with the next player in turn order, each player takes a random card from the discard pile and puts it into their hand. Put the rest of the discard pile onto the bottom of the deck in a random order.

name: Benevolence
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn if it doesn't share a color with any of your moods.

name: Charity
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn.

name: Chivalry
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [5] if you didn't go first this round.

name: Conviction
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose a mood. Its player puts it on the bottom of the deck and draws a card.

name: Courage
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose up to two players. For each chosen player, put one of their moods with a value of [1] or more into the discard pile.

name: Dignity
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may discard a card from your hand with a [0], [1], [2], or [3] in its top right corner. If you do, this mood's value becomes [5].

name: Discipline
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [3] if there are two or more black and/or red moods.

name: Disillusionment
rulesText: After playing this mood — Starting with the next player in turn order, each player may choose a color. Put each other mood that shares one of those colors into the discard pile.

name: Encouragement
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose a mood with dice in its lower left corner. While in play — The chosen mood uses the dice total in its top right corner or lower left corner, whichever is higher, to determine its value.

name: Faith
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may discard a green or blue card from your hand. If you do, suppress any mood. It remains suppressed for as long as you have this mood. (While suppressed, that mood has a value of [0]. Turn it sideways to keep track.)

name: Friendliness
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn if it has a [0], [1], [2], or [3] in its top right corner.

name: Guilt
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose one: • Suppress a black or red mood. It remains suppressed for as long as you have this mood. (While suppressed, that mood has a value of [0]. Turn it sideways to keep track.) • Suppress all black and red moods. Those moods remain suppressed for as long as you have this mood.

name: Honor
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose a player. While in play — The chosen player goes first each round regardless of who won the previous round. (This doesn't change who draws a card.)

name: Idealism
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn. While in play — For each of your moods with dice in its lower left corner, use the dice total in its top right corner or lower left corner, whichever is higher, to determine its value.

name: Kindness
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn if it has a [1], [2], or [3] in its top right corner.

name: Loyalty
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if there are two or more green and/or blue moods.

name: Meekness
rulesText: After playing this mood — Suppress all moods with a value of [2] or more. Those moods remain suppressed for as long as you have this mood. (While suppressed, those moods have a value of [0]. Turn them sideways to keep track.)

name: Pacifism
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose up to two players. For each chosen player, suppress one of their moods. It remains suppressed for as long as you have this mood. (While suppressed, that mood has a value of [0]. Turn it sideways to keep track.)

name: Patience
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [1] if you played it this round.

name: Pride
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose a player with more moods than you (moods are cards in play). If you do, you may keep playing additional moods this turn until you have as many moods as the chosen player.

name: Repentance
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose a number. If you do, suppress all other moods with the chosen value. They remain suppressed until the end of this round. (While suppressed, those moods have a value of [0]. Turn them sideways to keep track.)

name: Scorn
rulesText: After playing this mood — Suppress any mood. It remains suppressed until the end of this round. (While suppressed, that mood has a value of [0]. Turn it sideways to keep track.) While in play — Each time you play another mood, you may suppress a mood that shares a color with the mood you played. It remains suppressed until the end of this round.

name: Shame
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, suppress all other moods that share a color with the discarded card. Those moods remain suppressed for as long as you have this mood. (While suppressed, those moods have a value of [0]. Turn them sideways to keep track.)

name: Validation
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn. While in play — Each time you play another mood with a [0] or [1] in its top right corner, you may play an additional mood this turn.

name: Ambivalence
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [3] if there are two or more red and/or green moods.

name: Anxiety
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose up to two players. For each chosen player, put one of their moods with an odd value into their hand.

name: Avoidance
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose left or right. Each player chooses one of their moods and gives it to the next player in the chosen direction. (Moods are cards in play.)

name: Bashfulness
rulesText: After playing this mood — After scoring this round, if you won the round, put this mood on the bottom of the deck and draw a card.

name: Confusion
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose left or right. Each player chooses a card from their hand and gives it to the next player in the chosen direction.

name: Creativity
rulesText: You may play this card as a copy of any mood. (Treat this mood as if it were an exact copy of that printed card, including dice, color, and abilities. The copied traits last for as long as this mood is in play.)

name: Curiosity
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose a player. If you do, that player reveals a random card from their hand. If the revealed card shares a color with any mood, this mood's value becomes [6].

name: Denial
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose two other moods. If the two chosen moods share a color or have the same value, put them into their players' hands.

name: Disorientation
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose a number. If you do, put all other moods with the chosen value into their players' hands.

name: Doubt
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may reveal any number of cards from your hand and put them on the bottom of the deck, then draw that many cards. During the next round, players can't play moods that share a color with any of the revealed cards.

name: Duplicity
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn. While in play — Each time you play another mood, you may have that mood's "After playing this mood" effect happen an additional time. (If that effect involves a choice, different choices can be made each time.)

name: Fear
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put another one of your moods into your hand. You may play an additional mood this turn.

name: Fickleness
rulesText: After playing this mood — Calculate the most common color or colors among all moods. Put all moods other than this one that share one of those colors into their players' hands.

name: Guile
rulesText: To play this card — Discard two cards from your hand. (If you can't do that, you can't play this card.) After playing this mood — Choose one of your opponents' moods. It becomes yours.

name: Hesitation
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose one: • Put a red or green mood into its player's hand. • Put all red and green moods into their players' hands.

name: Imagination
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose a color. While in play — All moods are the chosen color and no other colors.

name: Indecisiveness
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose any number of opponents who each have two or more moods. Each chosen player puts a random one of their moods into their hand.

name: Insecurity
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn. If you do, after scoring, put that mood into your hand (if it's still in play).

name: Neurosis
rulesText: To play this card — Put one or more of your moods into your hand. (If you can't do that, you can't play this card.)

name: Obsession
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if there are two or more white and/or black moods.

name: Panic
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose up to two players. For each chosen player, put one of their moods into their hand. You can't put this mood into your hand this way.

name: Rationalization
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose one: • Put your hand on the bottom of the deck, then draw that many cards. • Choose left or right. Simultaneously, each player gives their hand to the next player in the chosen direction.

name: Regret
rulesText: To play this card — Put two of your moods into your hand. (If you can't do that, you can't play this card.) After playing this mood — Put an opponent's mood into your hand.

name: Sneakiness
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose an opponent. This round, after scoring, swap your score with that player before determining who wins the round.

name: Worry
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put one of your white or black moods into your hand. If you do, put up to two moods other than this one each with a value of [3] or less into their players' hands.

name: Ambition
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, you may play an additional mood this turn.

name: Angst
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put one of your blue or red moods into the discard pile. If you do, you may play an additional mood this turn from the discard pile. (You can play the card you just put into the discard pile if you want.)

name: Betrayal
rulesText: After playing this mood — Give one of your moods to another player. After scoring, that mood becomes yours again (if it's still in play).

name: Bitterness
rulesText: After playing this mood — Calculate the most common color or colors among all moods. Put all other moods that share one of those colors into the discard pile.

name: Condescension
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may give a card from your hand to another player. If you do, this mood's value becomes [6].

name: Contempt
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose one: • Put a green or white mood into the discard pile. • Put all green and white moods into the discard pile.

name: Corruption
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose one: • Put up to two cards from the discard pile on the bottom of the deck, then draw that many cards. • The winner of the current round wins two rounds instead of one. (Each losing player still draws only one card.)

name: Cruelty
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose any number of opponents who each have two or more moods. Each chosen opponent puts a random one of their moods into the discard pile. (Moods are cards in play.)

name: Cynicism
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put a card from the discard pile into an opponent's hand. If you do, this mood's value becomes [6].

name: Disgust
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [3] if there are two or more green and/or white moods.

name: Envy
rulesText: To play this card — Put one of your moods into the discard pile. (If you can't do that, you can't play this card. Moods are cards in play.) While in play — This mood's value increases by [1] for each mood your moodiest opponent has.

name: Grief
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play up to two additional moods this turn from the discard pile.

name: Hate
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put any mood on the bottom of the deck. If you do, draw a card. (Moods are cards in play.)

name: Intimidation
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose another player. If you do, that player reveals a card from their hand and puts it into your hand. You may play it as an additional mood this turn.

name: Malice
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose any player who has two or more moods (moods are cards in play). That player chooses two of their moods. Put those moods and all other moods that share a color with either of them into the discard pile.

name: Melancholy
rulesText: While in play — You may play moods from the discard pile as though they were in your hand.

name: Misery
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][2] if there are two or more cards in the discard pile that share a color.

name: Paranoia
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose a player with one or more cards in their hand. If you do, that player reveals a random card from their hand and puts it on the bottom of the deck, then you draw a card.

name: Pity
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if there are two or more blue and/or red moods.

name: Rejection
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose two other moods. If the two chosen moods share a color or have the same value, put them into the discard pile.

name: Sadness
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value increases by [1] for each card in the discard pile.

name: Self-Loathing
rulesText: To play this card — Put one or more of your moods into the discard pile. (If you can't do that, you can't play this card. Moods are cards in play.)

name: Spite
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose up to two players. For each chosen player, put one of their moods with an even value into the discard pile. ([0] is even.)

name: Superiority
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][1] if you have more moods than each other player. (Moods are cards in play.)

name: Suspicion
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose any number of players. Each chosen player discards a card from their hand.

name: Vanity
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value increases by [1] for each of your moods (including itself). If there are no cards in your hand, this mood's value instead increases by [2] for each of your moods (including itself).

name: Anger
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put any number of moods with total value [3] or less into the discard pile.

name: Animosity
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [5] if any opponent has three or more cards in hand.

name: Arrogance
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose an opponent. If you do, they choose one of their white or blue moods and it becomes yours. After this mood is no longer in play, give the mood you took back to them (if you still have it).

name: Bravado
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put one of your other moods into the discard pile (moods are cards in play). If you do, you may play an additional mood this turn.

name: Chaos
rulesText: After playing this mood — Shuffle all moods together. Starting with you and going in turn order, deal those moods out one at a time to each player. (Moods may change players but "After playing this mood" effects won't happen again.)

name: Compulsion
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose another player. That player chooses a card from their hand and gives it to you.

name: Embarrassment
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may discard a card from your hand with a [1], [2], or [3] in its top right corner. If you do, this mood's value becomes [5].

name: Excitement
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if there are two or more black and/or green moods.

name: Exhilaration
rulesText: To play this card — Put one of your moods into the discard pile. (If you can't do that, you can't play this card. Moods are cards in play.) While in play — Score your moods an extra time.

name: Frustration
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [3] if there are two or more white and/or blue moods.

name: Fury
rulesText: After playing this mood — Each player chooses one of their highest value moods and puts it into the discard pile.

name: Glee
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if you played it this round.

name: Gluttony
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn. If you do, after scoring, put that mood into the discard pile (if it's still in play).

name: Hostility
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put one of your black or green moods into the discard pile. If you do, put up to two moods each with a value of [2] or less into the discard pile.

name: Infatuation
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put two of your other moods into the discard pile (moods are cards in play). If you do, this mood's value becomes [6][3].

name: Instability
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may choose two moods from the same opponent. If you do, they choose one of those moods and gives it to you, then you give them one of your moods.

name: Passion
rulesText: While in play — While scoring, you may score one of your opponents' moods as though it were yours. (They also still score it.)

name: Rage
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put all other moods with a value of [2] or less into the discard pile.

name: Rebellion
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose [0], [1], [2], or [3]. Put all other moods with the chosen value into the discard pile.

name: Recklessness
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may take one of your opponents' moods. If you do, after scoring, give the mood you took back to them (if you still have it). While in play — After scoring, put this mood on the bottom of the deck and draw a card.

name: Shock
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose up to two players. For each chosen player, put one of their moods with a value of [2] or less into the discard pile.

name: Stubbornness
rulesText: While in play — At the start of each of your turns, if another player has more moods than you, you may play an additional mood this turn. (Moods are cards in play.)

name: Thrill
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put any number of your other moods into your hand. If you do, you may play that many additional moods this turn.

name: Triumph
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [5] if you went first this round.

name: Wrath
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put all other moods into the discard pile.

name: Zeal
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put a card from your hand on the bottom of the deck. If you do, draw a card.

name: Awe
rulesText: After playing this mood — There is no scoring this round. No one wins or loses this round. You choose which player goes first next round. (This round, no one will draw a card or get Hurt Feelings, and "after scoring" effects won't happen.)

name: Bliss
rulesText: To play this card — Discard a card from your hand. (If you can't do that, you can't play this card.) While in play — Score each of your moods that share a color with the discarded card two extra times.

name: Celebration
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][1] if there are more colors among your moods than among each other player's moods.

name: Cheer
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may discard a card from your hand with a [0], [1], [2], or [3] in its top right corner. If you do, this mood's value becomes [5].

name: Delight
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may discard a card from your hand with a [1], [2], or [3] in its top right corner. If you do, this mood's value becomes [5].

name: Determination
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if there are three or more moods that share a color. (Moods are cards in play.)

name: Disregard
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [3] if there are two or more blue and/or black moods.

name: Eagerness
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn if it shares a color with one of your moods.

name: Enjoyment
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if there are two or more red and/or white moods.

name: Enthusiasm
rulesText: While in play — While scoring, you may score one of your moods an extra time.

name: Euphoria
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value increases by [1] for each mood (including itself and other players' moods).

name: Fascination
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may reveal a blue or black card from your hand and give it to another player. If you do, this mood's value becomes [6][1].

name: Fondness
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][1] if each player has three or more moods. (Moods are cards in play.)

name: Generosity
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose an opponent. They may play an additional mood on their next turn.

name: Grace
rulesText: While in play — During each of your turns (including the turn you play this mood), you may play an additional mood from the discard pile if it shares a color with one of your moods.

name: Happiness
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][2] if a player has both a red mood and a white mood.

name: Harmony
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood this turn from the discard pile.

name: Hope
rulesText: While in play — You may play an additional mood during each of your turns (including the turn you play this mood).

name: Joy
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may play an additional mood on your next turn.

name: Love
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][6] if there's a white mood, a blue mood, a black mood, a red mood, and a green mood (including this one).

name: Nostalgia
rulesText: After playing this mood — You may put a card from the discard pile into your hand. You may play an additional mood this turn.

name: Serenity
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if you have an even number of moods (including this one).

name: Sloth
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value increases by [1] for each card in your hand.

name: Tranquility
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6] if you have an odd number of moods (including this one).

name: Vulnerability
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][1] if a card was put into the discard pile this round.

name: Wonder
rulesText: After playing this mood — Choose a color. While in play — This mood's value increases by [1] for each mood of the chosen color and each card in the discard pile of the chosen color.

name: Love
rulesText: While in play — This mood's value is [6][6] if there's a white mood, a blue mood, a black mood, a red mood, and a green mood (including this one).

name: Hurt Feelings
rulesText: (In a 3+ player game, at the end of each round, the player with the lowest score this round gets Hurt Feelings for the next round. If there's a tie, whoever took a turn latest this round wins the tie.) This round, you may play an additional mood during your turn.
